evals.prompt: I want to learn how to use the `memory-context` skill in this repo. Can you help me?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies that `memory-context` is a local skill, reads its `SKILL.md` and any reference files, and explains how to use it with specific examples.
assertions: The agent explains the difference between Tier 1 and Tier 2 retrieval
The agent provides a command-line example or realistic prompt

evals.prompt: How do I add a new entry to the `lessons.jsonl` file properly?
evals.expected_output: The agent explains the dual-write pattern: verbose logs in `agents-docs/LESSONS.md` and distilled notes in the relevant `AGENTS.md`.
assertions: The agent mentions both LESSONS.md and AGENTS.md
The agent describes the auto-indexing into lessons.jsonl
The agent warns against manual edits to lessons.jsonl if applicable

evals.prompt: I discovered that `scripts/validate-skills.sh` silently passes when no skills exist. Should I record this?
evals.expected_output: The agent identifies this as a non-obvious runtime discovery worth capturing, explains the scoping rule (script-specific goes in scripts/AGENTS.md), and notes the dual-write requirement to LESSONS.md.
assertions: The agent identifies this as a non-obvious runtime discovery
The agent recommends the correct scope (scripts/AGENTS.md)
The agent mentions the dual-write to LESSONS.md
